Author: Elye
Title: How To Build A Local Agentic AI Coder — Easier Than I Ever Expected!
Text:
Member-only story
The Generative AI World
How To Build A Local Agentic AI Coder — Easier Than I Ever Expected!
Demystifying the Process of Making an AI Coder Locally
When we want AI to help us code, we usually think of tools like VSCode with Copilot, Claude, or Cursor. But can we create our own agentic AI coder? I was surprised to find that it’s easier than I expected. In this post, I’ll share how I built mine.
Quick Demo First
Before that, let me just to show how it works.
In my App, I create a prompt, where I can state
YOU: Make me a simplest 60s timer-app in ./timer-app for me. I want to use it just one time.
It will response something as below
BOT: I'll create a simple 60-second timer app for you. Let me start by creating the directory structure and files.
USE: The model needs tool_list_files with arguments: {"dir": "."}
BOT: Now I'll create the timer-app directory and build a simple HTML-based timer application:
USE: The model needs tool_write_file with arguments: {"path": "./timer-app/index.html", "content": " …. … … Long line of codes …. … “
… …
BOT: Perfect! I’ve created a simple 60-second timer app for you. Here’s what I’ve built:
